meta_title: Private GP Appointments in Preston | Same Day from £69
meta_description: Same-day private GP in Fulwood, Preston. 15, 30 and 60-minute slots. Adults from £69, children from £49. No referral needed. Dr Faizal Secretary.
hero_h1: A Private GP in Preston You Can Actually See Today
hero_sub: Same-day availability. 15 or 30-minute slots. No NHS referral needed to book.
hero_trust: Adults from £69 · Children from £49 · Prescription Writing Included
intro_quote: You finally get an appointment. You have three things to talk about. You leave having covered one.
intro_heading: Private GP Appointments in Fulwood, Preston
intro_para_1: A new symptom, a long-ignored concern, a second opinion, a prescription or referral — whatever brings you in, Dr Faizal Secretary has the time to address it properly at our Fulwood, Preston clinic.
intro_para_2: No NHS referral or registration required. Just book directly and be seen the same day.

faqs.question: Do I need to de-register from my NHS GP?
faqs.answer: No. We work entirely alongside the NHS. Keep your NHS GP — we're here for when you need to be seen sooner or need more time.

faqs.question: Can I get a same-day appointment?
faqs.answer: Yes, subject to availability. Call before midday and we'll usually get you in the same day — particularly on Fridays when we run a full clinic from 9am to 8pm. Other days are by appointment.

faqs.question: Can I book a telephone or video appointment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Telephone and video appointments are available for existing patients at £49 for 15 minutes. New patients are encouraged to attend in person for their first appointment.

faqs.question: What if I need to see a specialist?
faqs.answer: The doctor will write a referral letter as part of your appointment — no extra charge. He has established relationships with private specialists across the North West.

faqs.question: Are prescriptions included?
faqs.answer: The prescription writing is included — if the doctor decides you need medication, he writes the prescription as part of your appointment at no extra charge. You then take it to any UK pharmacy and pay only the dispensing cost there.

faqs.question: What conditions do you see?
faqs.answer: All general medical conditions — acute illness, chronic condition reviews, infections, skin problems, joint pain, repeat prescriptions, second opinions and more.
